prompt:
The rules layer of our game-lab teaching rig is being ported piece by piece to assembly so students can single-step it, and tic-tac-toe is next. I need the board evaluator — no bot, no search, just the two primitives everything else sits on. One file, tttwin.s: x86-64, GNU as, AT&T syntax, System V AMD64 ABI (args RDI, RSI; return RAX; RBX/RBP/R12-R15 callee-saved; if you call one routine from the other keep RSP 16-byte aligned at the call). No libc, no syscalls. End the file with `.section .note.GNU-stack,"",@progbits`.

Boards are 9 raw bytes, cells 0..8 row-major (0=top-left, 8=bottom-right), values 0 empty, 1 X, 2 O. Every byte the harness passes is in {0,1,2}, but boards are otherwise arbitrary — impossible positions included (both sides holding lines, wrong move parity, all nine cells X). The evaluator must be total over that whole space; the suite literally enumerates all 3^9 boards.

    int  ttt_status(const unsigned char *board);
    long ttt_moves(const unsigned char *board, unsigned char *out);

- ttt_status: 1 if X holds any of the eight lines (three rows, three columns, two diagonals), else 2 if O holds one, else 3 if no cell is empty, else 0 (ongoing). Checking X before O on doubly-won boards is pinned — don't reorder it. A win on a full board is a win, not a draw.
- ttt_moves: if ttt_status is anything but 0, write nothing and return 0. Otherwise write the indices of the empty cells into out in ascending order, one byte each, and return how many you wrote. Never store past the returned count — the harness sentinels the tail of the buffer and checks.

Verification is differential and exhaustive: test_tttwin.c has an independent C implementation, pinned spot boards (including the both-lines and win-on-full-board cases), and the full 19683-board walk with category totals pinned from an oracle. bash test_tttwin.sh builds `gcc -Wall -Wextra -Werror -O2 -o runner test_tttwin.c tttwin.s` and runs it. Don't modify either test file.
notes: 7A1 item 3. TTT rotation: py-tictactoe (engine+minimax CLI), go-ttt-server (TCP protocol), zsh-ttt (referee CLI), c-tttbit (bitboard representation kernel); this one is a raw byte-array total evaluator + move lister over the full 3^9 space — no bitboards (explicitly differentiates vs c-tttbit's two-mask API), no game loop. Pinned totals (11093/4435/4123/32, 40075 moves) computed by an independent Python oracle.
